agent.name: Oryele Agent
agent.greeting: Hi — I’m Oryele Agent. Ask me about the platform, digital workforce, pricing, industries, trust, or how to request a briefing.
agent.fallback: I don’t have a precise match for that yet. Try asking about pricing, digital workforce, communications, trust/security, or request a briefing. You can also email info@oryele.com or visit /contact.
suggested: How do I request a demo?

faqs.question: What is Oryele?
faqs.answer: Oryele is an Enterprise Execution Platform that orchestrates digital workers, communications, documents, knowledge, and AI across your organization. It helps teams run the business—not chase workflows—by coordinating people, systems, and governed automation in one layer.

faqs.question: Who is Oryele for?
faqs.answer: Oryele focuses on professional services organizations with repetitive operational follow-up—especially accounting firms, law firms, insurance agencies, and service operations teams that need governed intake, document collection, communications, and workflow completion.

faqs.question: What are Oryele pricing plans?
faqs.answer:
Oryele offers three plans:
• Foundation — $399/mo: core platform, essential workflows, client portal, email support, 25,000 OCR pages/month.
• Professional — $999/mo (Preferred): everything in Foundation plus advanced workflows, digital worker expansion, priority support, email/SMS/voice/calendar, 250,000 OCR pages/month.
• Enterprise — custom (call sales): custom users/storage/OCR, SSO/private deployment options, SLA and dedicated success, advanced governance.

faqs.question: What is the Digital Workforce?
faqs.answer: Oryele Digital Workforce deploys specialized AI-powered workers for operational tasks, including Client Intake, Document Collection, Reception, Scheduling, Knowledge, and Compliance workers. They capture information, request missing items, send reminders, coordinate calendars, answer from approved knowledge, and maintain audit evidence.

faqs.question: What communications channels does Oryele support?
faqs.answer: Oryele unifies email, SMS, voice/telephone, and calendar automation inside governed workflows. You can send requests, reminders, confirmations, and updates with consent handling, preference tracking, and message history for auditability.

faqs.question: What does the Platform include?
faqs.answer: The Oryele Platform is a governed execution layer with Execution Core, Digital Workforce, Workflow Engine, Communications, Governance, and Analytics. Work is routed, assigned, executed, measured, and controlled with ownership and audit trails.

faqs.question: How does Oryele handle security and trust?
faqs.answer: Oryele Trust Center controls include role-based access, audit trails, approvals, legal hold, and retention designed for enterprise oversight. The platform emphasizes auditability, access management, and operational governance for professional services.

faqs.question: How do I contact Oryele or request a demo?
faqs.answer: Request a briefing or demo on the Contact page. You can also email info@oryele.com for product inquiries, sales@oryele.com for sales, visit oryele.com, or use the app at app.oryele.com. Address: Oryele LLC, 1021 Broad St. #1129, Shrewsbury, NJ 07702.

faqs.question: What is Oryele’s SMS policy?
faqs.answer: Oryele supports consent-based SMS for appointments, document requests, reminders, and updates. Opt-in information is not sold or shared with third parties for marketing. Messaging is governed by the SMS Policy, Terms, and Privacy Policy.

faqs.question: Where can I find privacy and legal information?
faqs.answer: Privacy details are in the Privacy Policy. Related documents include Terms & Conditions, SMS Policy, and Cookie Policy. For privacy questions, contact info@oryele.com.

faqs.question: How does Oryele help accounting firms?
faqs.answer: For CPA and accounting firms, Oryele supports client intake, document collection, reminders, engagement tracking, and workflow visibility—reducing manual chasing during tax season and improving completion across client work.

faqs.question: How does Oryele help law firms?
faqs.answer: For legal organizations, Oryele supports matter intake, client communications, document requests, scheduling, and approval workflows with auditability across documents and communication records.

faqs.question: How does Oryele help insurance agencies?
faqs.answer: For insurance organizations, Oryele supports new business intake, policyholder updates, claims support workflows, and renewal follow-up with better visibility from request to completion.

faqs.question: Where do I log in?
faqs.answer: Use the Login button in the site navigation or go to https://app.oryele.com for the client portal and application environment.

faqs.question: What resources are available?
faqs.answer: The Resources section includes white papers and product guidance for accounting, legal, and insurance organizations, plus brochure materials for the Enterprise Execution Layer.

faqs.question: Does Oryele include OCR?
faqs.answer: Yes. OCR is part of the governed execution platform. Plan allowances include 25,000 OCR pages/month on Foundation, 250,000 on Professional, and custom volume on Enterprise.

faqs.question: What can Oryele Agent help with?
faqs.answer: I’m Oryele Agent. I can explain the platform, digital workforce, pricing, industries, trust/security, communications, and how to request a briefing—using Oryele’s public website content. Ask about a feature, plan, or use case.

pages.title: Privacy Policy — Oryele
pages.content: Privacy Policy — Oryele Legal Privacy Policy Oryele LLC · 1021 Broad St. #1129, Shrewsbury, NJ 07702 · Effective date: June 22, 2026 · Last updated: June 22, 2026 1. Who we are Oryele LLC ("Oryele", "we", "us", or "our") is a technology company headquartered at 1021 Broad St. #1129, Shrewsbury, NJ 07702. We provide an AI-powered digital workforce platform for professional service organizations, including accounting firms, law firms, and insurance organizations. This Privacy Policy describes how we collect, use, disclose, and protect information in connection with our platform, website, and related services (collectively, the "Services"). 2. Information we collect We collect information in the following ways: Information you provide directly — such as your name, company name, email address, phone number, and business information when you register, request a briefing, or contact us. Client and end-user information — when accounting firms and other customers ("Clients") use our platform, they may submit information about their own clients and personnel. We process this information on behalf of our Clients as a data processor. Usage and technical data — including IP addresses, browser type, pages visited, referring URLs, and device information collected automatically when you use our Services. Communications — including emails, SMS messages, and voice call records processed through our platform in the ordinary course of delivering our Services. 3. How we use information We use collected information to: Provide, operate, and improve our platform and Services Send transactional communications including appointment confirmations, reminders, document requests, and notifications on behalf of our Clients Respond to inquiries and provide customer support Ensure the security and integrity of our platform Comply with applicable legal obligations Analyze usage to improve our Services 4. SMS and voice communications Oryele may send SMS notifications and voice communications on behalf of Clients to their authorized end users. These messages are transactional and service-related, including appointment reminders, scheduling confirmations, document requests, workflow notifications, engagement or case status updates, and account-related service notices. SMS consent is optional. End users opt in through the Oryele Client Portal during onboarding by manually selecting an unchecked SMS consent checkbox. Users are not automatically enrolled, and consent is not a condition of purchasing goods or services. Oryele does not sell SMS consent information. SMS opt-in data and phone numbers are not shared with third parties or affiliates for marketing or promotional purposes. Message frequency varies based on appointment schedules, document requests, and workflow activity. Message and data rates may apply. To opt out of SMS communications, reply STOP to any message. For assistance, reply HELP or contact info@oryele.com . For additional messaging details and a public example of our opt-in workflow, please see our SMS Policy . 5. How we share information We do not sell personal information. We may share information in the following limited circumstances: With our Clients — we

pages.title: SMS Policy — Oryele
pages.content: SMS Policy — Oryele Messaging Compliance SMS Policy Oryele LLC · 1021 Broad St. #1129, Shrewsbury, NJ 07702 · Effective date: June 26, 2026 · Last updated: June 26, 2026 Oryele SMS Communications Oryele sends transactional, service-related SMS communications on behalf of professional service firms and other business customers that use the Oryele platform. Messages may relate to appointments, scheduling, client document requests, workflow notifications, engagement or case status updates, and account-related service notices. No marketing messages are sent through this SMS program. How users opt in Users opt in through the Oryele Client Portal during first-time onboarding or account setup. The opt-in workflow is as follows: A user is invited by their accounting firm, law firm, insurance agency, or other business service provider using Oryele. The user accesses the Oryele Client Portal. Before entering the portal, the user is shown an SMS Communications consent screen. The SMS checkbox is unchecked by default. The user must actively check the box to consent to SMS communications. The user may continue to the portal without opting in. SMS consent is optional. Example of the Oryele Client Portal SMS consent screen presented during onboarding. The checkbox is unchecked by default and requires active user selection. Consent language shown to users The Oryele Client Portal displays consent language substantially similar to the following: SMS Communications By checking this box, I expressly consent to receive SMS messages from Oryele on behalf of my service provider regarding appointments, scheduling, document requests, workflow notifications, account updates, and other service-related communications. Message frequency varies. Message and data rates may apply. Reply STOP to opt out. Reply HELP for help. Consent is not required to purchase goods or services. Types of messages Appointment reminders Scheduling confirmations, changes, and cancellations Client document requests Workflow notifications and reminders Engagement, case, account, or service-related status updates Message frequency Message frequency varies depending on appointment schedules, document requests, and workflow activity. Message and data rates Message and data rates may apply depending on the user's mobile carrier and plan. Opt-out instructions Users may opt out of SMS communications at any time by replying STOP to any SMS message. After opting out, the user will receive a confirmation message and no further SMS messages will be sent unless the user opts in again. Help instructions Users may reply HELP to any SMS message for assistance or contact Oryele at info@oryele.com . Privacy and sharing Oryele does not sell SMS opt-in information. SMS consent, phone numbers, and messaging data are not shared with third parties or affiliates for marketing or promotional purposes. See our Privacy Policy for more information. Terms Use of Oryele messaging is also governed by our Terms & Conditions . For questions about SMS communications, please contact: Oryele LLC 1021 Broad St. #1129 Shrewsbury, NJ 07702 Email: info@oryele.com

pages.title: Terms & Conditions — Oryele
pages.content: Terms & Conditions — Oryele Legal Terms & Conditions Oryele LLC · 1021 Broad St. #1129, Shrewsbury, NJ 07702 · Effective date: June 22, 2026 · Last updated: June 22, 2026 1. Agreement to terms These Terms and Conditions ("Terms") govern your access to and use of the Oryele Digital Workforce Platform, website, and related services (collectively, the "Services") provided by Oryele LLC ("Oryele", "we", "us", or "our"), a company incorporated in New Jersey with its principal office at 1021 Broad St. #1129, Shrewsbury, NJ 07702. By accessing or using our Services, you agree to be bound by these Terms. If you do not agree, you may not use our Services. 2. Description of services Oryele provides an AI-powered digital workforce platform designed for professional service organizations. Our Services include client intake automation, document collection management, scheduling and appointment coordination, SMS and voice communications, reminder engine functionality, and related workflow automation tools. Services are provided on a software-as-a-service basis to registered business customers ("Clients"). 3. Eligibility and accounts Our Services are intended for business use by organizations and their authorized representatives. By using our Services, you represent that: You are at least 18 years of age You have the authority to bind your organization to these Terms All information you provide is accurate and current You will maintain the security and confidentiality of your account credentials You are responsible for all activity that occurs under your account. 4. Messaging program terms Oryele sends transactional SMS and voice communications on behalf of Clients to their authorized end users as part of the Services. The following terms apply to the Oryele messaging program: Program name: Oryele Digital Workforce Platform Program description: Transactional appointment reminders, scheduling confirmations, reschedule and cancellation notices, client document requests, workflow notifications, case or engagement status updates, and account-related service notices sent on behalf of professional service firms Opt-in method: During first-time onboarding in the Oryele Client Portal, users may actively check an unchecked SMS consent checkbox. Users are not automatically enrolled. Consent is optional and is not a condition of purchasing goods or services Message frequency: Varies based on appointment schedule, client engagement activity, document requests, and workflow events Message and data rates: Message and data rates may apply depending on your mobile carrier and plan Opt-out: Reply STOP to any SMS message to unsubscribe. You will receive a confirmation message and no further SMS messages will be sent unless you opt in again Help: Reply HELP to any SMS message or contact us at info@oryele.com for assistance Privacy: SMS opt-in data and consent are not sold and are not shared with third parties or affiliates for marketing or promotional purposes Supported carriers: All major US carriers. Carrier support may vary and carriers are not liable for delayed or undelivered messages For a public explanation of the opt-in workflow and an example consent screen, see our
